title: Incidents do not arrive as clean bug reports.
body: RePro turns screenshots, logs, PDFs, stack traces, and pressure into a verified, approval-gated pull request with readable proof.

title: The dashboard keeps live work legible.
body: Runs, approvals, confidence, and recent activity stay in one control surface so teams can see what the system is doing and what happens next.

title: Starting a run is structured, not magical.
body: Operators choose an allowlisted repository, add incident context, and decide whether to stop at dry-run or continue through apply-and-verify.

title: Policy is part of the product.
body: Repository-specific commands, outbound rules, package managers, and CI hooks stay visible and reviewable so the workflow can scale without dropping its safety model.

title: The trace is the trust layer.
body: Evidence parsing, suspect files, sandbox execution, and verification status update in real time as a readable narrative of what the system actually did.

title: The detail page turns verification into evidence.
body: Confidence, reproducibility, observability coverage, replay actions, and trace links sit beside the underlying evidence before approval is requested.

title: Every run becomes operational memory.
body: Run history, approval records, policy inventory, and incident correlation make the control plane durable long after the live fix is done.

title: Voice feeds the same governed engine.
body: The operator can start from speech, pick a repository, and hand the work to the same policy-aware execution loop.

title: More than a coding agent.
body: Verification-first software reliability with multimodal intake, sandboxed execution, human approval at the edge, and auditable proof at the end.
